rationale: The citing paper references Donkers et al. (2020) only in grouped background citations, mainly to note that defaults are often treated as a passive/heuristic bias and to give an example where default main effects can be weak or absent. The paper does not engage that study's specific sequential-choice framework, methods, or results in its own model development or empirical design. There is no distinct functional role assigned to this specific citation beyond general literature positioning.
